reasoning:
For the first date (Feb 22), the FIDE rankings update on the 1st of each month. It is virtually certain that the Wikipedia page on Feb 22 will reflect the same FIDE list (February 2026) as on Feb 15, meaning Hou Yifan's rank will remain 1. 

For the second date (March 17), the FIDE rankings will have updated for March. Hou Yifan's rating is 2596 and the #2 player Zhu Jiner is 2578, an 18-point gap. A recently published Chessbase article from Feb 3, 2026, confirms Hou Yifan has been active during the Chinese League, ensuring she won't drop off the list due to inactivity in March. Zhu Jiner's next confirmed major tournament is the Prague Chess Festival, which runs from Feb 24 to March 6; because it ends in March, it will not be rated until April 1. Given the difficulty of bridging an 18-point gap in a single month and Zhu Jiner's lack of known classical tournaments finishing before the February 28 rating cutoff, Hou Yifan is highly likely to remain the #1 ranked woman on the March list. There's a slight risk of unforeseen tournaments or unannounced updates, hence the 94% estimate.
